23	// fakeExecuteBeadGit is a mock implementation of executeBeadGitOps for tests.
24	type fakeExecuteBeadGit struct {
25		mu sync.Mutex
26	
27		// mainHeadRev is returned by HeadRev/ResolveRev for the main working dir.
28		mainHeadRev string
29		// headRevSeq, when set, is returned in order for successive main-dir HeadRev calls.
30		headRevSeq []string
31		headRevIdx int
32		// wtHeadRev is returned by HeadRev for worktree paths (after agent run).
33		wtHeadRev string
34		// wtDirty is returned by IsDirty for worktree paths.
35		wtDirty bool
36		// synthRev, if set, is applied as wtHeadRev when SynthesizeCommit is called.
37		synthRev string
38		// wtHeadRevErr, if set, is returned by HeadRev for worktree paths.
39		wtHeadRevErr error
40		dirty        bool
41		mergeErr     error
42		updateRefErr error
43	
44		addedWTs   []string
45		addedWTRev string
46		removedWTs []string
47		refs       map[string]string // ref -> sha recorded by UpdateRef
48		worktrees  []string          // paths returned by WorktreeList
49	
50		mergeCalls int
51		mergeRev   string
52	}

54	func (f *fakeExecuteBeadGit) HeadRev(dir string) (string, error) {
55		f.mu.Lock()
56		defer f.mu.Unlock()
57		if strings.Contains(dir, agent.ExecuteBeadWtPrefix) {
58			if f.wtHeadRevErr != nil {
59				return "", f.wtHeadRevErr
60			}
61			return f.wtHeadRev, nil
62		}
63		if len(f.headRevSeq) > 0 {
64			idx := f.headRevIdx
65			if idx >= len(f.headRevSeq) {
66				idx = len(f.headRevSeq) - 1
67			}
68			rev := f.headRevSeq[idx]
69			f.headRevIdx++
70			return rev, nil
71		}
72		return f.mainHeadRev, nil
73	}
74	
75	func (f *fakeExecuteBeadGit) ResolveRev(dir, rev string) (string, error) {
76		f.mu.Lock()
77		defer f.mu.Unlock()
78		return f.mainHeadRev, nil
79	}

81	func (f *fakeExecuteBeadGit) IsDirty(dir string) (bool, error) {
82		f.mu.Lock()
83		defer f.mu.Unlock()
84		if strings.Contains(dir, agent.ExecuteBeadWtPrefix) {
85			return f.wtDirty, nil
86		}
87		return f.dirty, nil

90	func (f *fakeExecuteBeadGit) WorktreeAdd(dir, wtPath, rev string) error {
91		f.mu.Lock()
92		defer f.mu.Unlock()
93		f.addedWTs = append(f.addedWTs, wtPath)
94		f.addedWTRev = rev
95		if err := os.MkdirAll(wtPath, 0o755); err != nil {
96			return err
97		}
98		beadFile := filepath.Join(dir, ".ddx", "beads.jsonl")
99		if _, err := os.Stat(beadFile); err == nil {
100			if err := copyTestFile(beadFile, filepath.Join(wtPath, ".ddx", "beads.jsonl")); err != nil {
101				return err
102			}
103		}
104		docsDir := filepath.Join(dir, "docs")
105		if _, err := os.Stat(docsDir); err == nil {
106			if err := copyTree(docsDir, filepath.Join(wtPath, "docs")); err != nil {
107				return err
108			}
109		}
110		return nil
111	}

129	func (f *fakeExecuteBeadGit) SynthesizeCommit(dir, msg string) (bool, error) {
130		f.mu.Lock()
131		defer f.mu.Unlock()

133			f.wtHeadRev = f.synthRev
134			return true, nil
135		}

172	// fakeAgentRunner is a minimal mock agent runner for execute-bead tests.
173	type fakeAgentRunner struct {
174		result *agent.Result
175		err    error
176		last   agent.RunOptions
177		// sideEffect, when set, runs while the runner has the opts in hand. It
178		// is used to simulate runtime state the embedded agent harness would
179		// otherwise write (session logs, telemetry, etc.) so tests can assert
180		// where those files land.
181		sideEffect func(opts agent.RunOptions) error
182	}
183	
184	func (r *fakeAgentRunner) Run(opts agent.RunOptions) (*agent.Result, error) {
185		r.last = opts
186		if r.sideEffect != nil {
187			if err := r.sideEffect(opts); err != nil {
188				return nil, err
189			}
190		}
191		return r.result, r.err
192	}

194	// newExecuteBeadFactory builds a CommandFactory wired with the given fake git and agent runner.
195	func newExecuteBeadFactory(t *testing.T, git *fakeExecuteBeadGit, runner *fakeAgentRunner) *CommandFactory {
196		t.Helper()
197		f := NewCommandFactory(t.TempDir())
198		seedDefaultExecuteBeads(t, f.WorkingDir)
199		f.AgentRunnerOverride = runner
200		f.executeBeadGitOverride = git
201		f.executeBeadOrchestratorGitOverride = git
202		f.executeBeadLandingAdvancerOverride = fakeLandingAdvancerFromGit(git)
203		return f
204	}

206	// fakeLandingAdvancerFromGit returns a LandingAdvancer callback that maps the
207	// fake git's Merge/UpdateRef semantics onto the coordinator-pattern advancer
208	// interface. Semantically, "mergeCalls" now means "number of times
209	// LandBeadResult invoked the advancer (attempted to advance the target
210	// branch)". Used by tests that were written against the old Merge() path so
211	// they continue to pass after the land-coordinator refactor.
212	func fakeLandingAdvancerFromGit(git *fakeExecuteBeadGit) func(res *agent.ExecuteBeadResult) (*agent.LandResult, error) {
213		return func(res *agent.ExecuteBeadResult) (*agent.LandResult, error) {
214			if err := git.Merge("", res.ResultRev); err != nil {
215				preserveRef := agent.PreserveRef(res.BeadID, res.BaseRev)
216				// Record the preserve ref in the fake's refs map so tests that
217				// assert git.refs[preserveRef] continue to pass.
218				_ = git.UpdateRef("", preserveRef, res.ResultRev)
219				return &agent.LandResult{
220					Status:      "preserved",
221					PreserveRef: preserveRef,
222					Reason:      "merge failed",
223				}, nil
224			}
225			return &agent.LandResult{Status: "landed", NewTip: res.ResultRev}, nil
226		}
227	}

229	func assertPreserveRef(t *testing.T, ref, beadID, baseRev string) {
230		t.Helper()
231		shortSHA := baseRev
232		if len(shortSHA) > 12 {
233			shortSHA = shortSHA[:12]
234		}
235		pattern := fmt.Sprintf(`^refs/ddx/iterations/%s/\d{8}T\d{6}Z-%s$`,
236			regexp.QuoteMeta(beadID), regexp.QuoteMeta(shortSHA))
237		require.Regexp(t, pattern, ref)
238	}

240	// runExecuteBead invokes the execute-bead command through the cobra tree and returns
241	// the parsed JSON result. It extracts the JSON object from the combined output,
242	// skipping any leading note/warning lines written to stderr.
243	func runExecuteBead(t *testing.T, f *CommandFactory, git *fakeExecuteBeadGit, beadID string, extraArgs ...string) agent.ExecuteBeadResult {
244		t.Helper()
245		root := f.NewRootCommand()
246		args := append([]string{"agent", "execute-bead", beadID, "--json"}, extraArgs...)
247		out, err := executeCommand(root, args...)
248		require.NoError(t, err, "execute-bead should not return an error; output: %s", out)
249		return parseExecuteBeadJSON(t, out)
250	}
251	
252	func parseExecuteBeadJSON(t *testing.T, out string) agent.ExecuteBeadResult {
253		t.Helper()
254		// Strip any non-JSON prefix lines (e.g. stderr notes written to the shared buffer).
255		jsonStart := strings.Index(out, "{")
256		require.NotEqual(t, -1, jsonStart, "output should contain JSON: %s", out)
257		jsonPart := out[jsonStart:]
258		var res agent.ExecuteBeadResult
259		dec := json.NewDecoder(bytes.NewBufferString(jsonPart))
260		require.NoError(t, dec.Decode(&res), "output should be valid JSON: %s", jsonPart)
261		return res
262	}

292	func copyTree(src, dst string) error {
293		return filepath.Walk(src, func(path string, info os.FileInfo, err error) error {
294			if err != nil {
295				return err
296			}
297			rel, err := filepath.Rel(src, path)
298			if err != nil {
299				return err
300			}
301			target := filepath.Join(dst, rel)
302			if info.IsDir() {
303				return os.MkdirAll(target, info.Mode())
304			}
305			if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(target), 0o755); err != nil {
306				return err
307			}
308			in, err := os.Open(path)
309			if err != nil {
310				return err
311			}
312			defer in.Close()
313			out, err := os.Create(target)
314			if err != nil {
315				return err
316			}
317			if _, err := io.Copy(out, in); err != nil {
318				out.Close()
319				return err
320			}
321			if err := out.Close(); err != nil {
322				return err
323			}
324			return os.Chmod(target, info.Mode())
325		})
326	}

355	// TestExecuteBeadMerge verifies that when merge succeeds the outcome is "merged".
356	func TestExecuteBeadMerge(t *testing.T) {
357		git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
358			mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111",
359			wtHeadRev:   "bbbb2222", // agent made a new commit
360			mergeErr:    nil,        // merge succeeds
361		}
362		runner := &fakeAgentRunner{result: &agent.Result{ExitCode: 0, Harness: "mock"}}
363		f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
364	
365		res := runExecuteBead(t, f, git, "my-bead")
366	
367		assert.Equal(t, "merged", res.Outcome)
368		assert.Equal(t, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess, res.Status)
369		assert.Equal(t, "aaaa1111", res.BaseRev)
370		assert.Equal(t, "bbbb2222", res.ResultRev)
371		assert.Empty(t, res.PreserveRef)
372		assert.Equal(t, "my-bead", res.BeadID)
373		assert.NotEmpty(t, res.SessionID)
374	
375		// Worktree should have been created and cleaned up.
376		require.Len(t, git.addedWTs, 1)
377		assert.Contains(t, git.addedWTs[0], agent.ExecuteBeadWtPrefix+"my-bead-")
378		require.Len(t, git.removedWTs, 1)
379		assert.Equal(t, git.addedWTs[0], git.removedWTs[0])
380		assert.Equal(t, 1, git.mergeCalls)
381		assert.Equal(t, "bbbb2222", git.mergeRev)
382	}

385	// the result is preserved under a hidden ref.

435	// TestExecuteBeadHiddenRefUniqueness verifies that two runs on the same bead-id
436	// produce distinct preserve refs (concurrent hidden-ref uniqueness).
437	func TestExecuteBeadHiddenRefUniqueness(t *testing.T) {
438		makeRun := func(ts time.Time) agent.ExecuteBeadResult {
439			oldNow := agent.NowFunc
440			agent.NowFunc = func() time.Time { return ts }
441			defer func() { agent.NowFunc = oldNow }()
442	
443			git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
444				mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111",
445				wtHeadRev:   "eeee5555",
446				mergeErr:    fmt.Errorf("diverged"),
447			}
448			runner := &fakeAgentRunner{result: &agent.Result{ExitCode: 0}}
449			f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
450			return runExecuteBead(t, f, git, "shared-bead")
451		}
452	
453		res1 := makeRun(time.Date(2026, 4, 10, 0, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC))
454		res2 := makeRun(time.Date(2026, 4, 10, 0, 0, 1, 0, time.UTC))
455	
456		assert.NotEqual(t, res1.PreserveRef, res2.PreserveRef,
457			"concurrent runs must produce distinct preserve refs")
458		assertPreserveRef(t, res1.PreserveRef, "shared-bead", "aaaa1111")
459		assertPreserveRef(t, res2.PreserveRef, "shared-bead", "aaaa1111")
460	}

524	func TestExecuteBeadSynthesizesPromptAndArtifacts(t *testing.T) {
525		workDir := t.TempDir()
526		seedExecuteBead(t, workDir, &bead.Bead{
527			ID:          "my-bead",
528			Title:       "Improve execute-bead prompt synthesis",
529			Status:      bead.StatusOpen,
530			Priority:    0,
531			IssueType:   bead.DefaultType,
532			Description: "Replace the bare fallback prompt with deterministic bead context.",
533			Acceptance:  "Prompt contains bead context and governing references.",
534			Labels:      []string{"area:agent", "phase:build"},
535			Extra:       map[string]any{"spec-id": "FEAT-006"},
536		})
537		specPath := filepath.Join(workDir, "docs", "feature.md")
538		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(specPath), 0o755))
539		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(specPath, []byte(`---
540	ddx:
541	  id: FEAT-006
542	---
543	# Agent Service
544	`), 0o644))
545	
546		git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
547			mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111",
548			wtHeadRev:   "bbbb2222",
549		}
550		runner := &fakeAgentRunner{result: &agent.Result{ExitCode: 0, Harness: "mock"}}
551		f := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
552		seedDefaultExecuteBeads(t, workDir)
553		f.AgentRunnerOverride = runner
554		f.executeBeadGitOverride = git
555		f.executeBeadOrchestratorGitOverride = git
556		f.executeBeadLandingAdvancerOverride = fakeLandingAdvancerFromGit(git)
557	
558		res := runExecuteBead(t, f, git, "my-bead")
559	
560		require.NotEmpty(t, runner.last.PromptFile)
561		require.FileExists(t, runner.last.PromptFile)
562		promptRaw, err := os.ReadFile(runner.last.PromptFile)
563		require.NoError(t, err)
564		promptText := string(promptRaw)
565		assert.Contains(t, promptText, "Improve execute-bead prompt synthesis")
566		assert.Contains(t, promptText, "Replace the bare fallback prompt")
567		assert.Contains(t, promptText, "Prompt contains bead context and governing references.")
568		assert.Contains(t, promptText, "docs/feature.md")
569		assert.NotContains(t, promptText, "Work on bead my-bead.")
570	
571		require.NotEmpty(t, res.ExecutionDir)

948	// execute-bead's model option: the value supplied via ExecuteBeadOptions.Model
949	// is passed verbatim to the runner, and an empty value is not silently replaced

952	// "z-ai/glm-5.1") when the caller did not request one — the case the agent
953	// harness resolves from ~/.config/agent/config.yaml must be preserved by
954	// ExecuteBead handing the runner an empty Model so the harness's own
955	// resolution chain runs.

969				"runner must receive an empty Model when no --model flag is provided; "+

1131	// TestExecuteBeadEmbeddedAgentStateRedirected verifies that when execute-bead
1132	// invokes the embedded-agent harness, its session/telemetry runtime state is
1133	// redirected into a DDx-owned directory inside the execution bundle instead
1134	// of being written at the worktree root. Regression guard for ddx-cba2dc64.
1135	func TestExecuteBeadEmbeddedAgentStateRedirected(t *testing.T) {
1136		git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
1137			mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111cafe",
1138			wtHeadRev:   "bbbb2222beef",
1139		}
1140	
1141		// Snapshot of wtPath root contents captured during Run so the assertion
1142		// can run before ExecuteBead removes the worktree on return.
1143		var wtPathDuringRun string
1144		var wtRootBefore []string
1145		var wtRootAfter []string
1146		var sessionLogDirSeen string
1147		simulatedSessionFile := "agent-embedded-session.jsonl"
1148	
1149		runner := &fakeAgentRunner{
1150			result: &agent.Result{ExitCode: 0, Harness: "agent"},
1151			sideEffect: func(opts agent.RunOptions) error {
1152				wtPathDuringRun = opts.WorkDir
1153				sessionLogDirSeen = opts.SessionLogDir
1154	
1155				// Capture worktree root listing before simulating writes.
1156				entries, err := os.ReadDir(opts.WorkDir)
1157				if err != nil {
1158					return err
1159				}
1160				for _, e := range entries {
1161					wtRootBefore = append(wtRootBefore, e.Name())
1162				}
1163	
1164				// Simulate the embedded-agent harness writing runtime state.
1165				// It MUST land in opts.SessionLogDir, not opts.WorkDir. If the
1166				// execute-bead wiring is broken and SessionLogDir is empty or the
1167				// worktree root, this write will land at the worktree root and
1168				// the post-check below will catch it.
1169				if opts.SessionLogDir == "" {
1170					return fmt.Errorf("embedded agent runner received empty SessionLogDir; runtime state would land at worktree root")
1171				}
1172				if err := os.MkdirAll(opts.SessionLogDir, 0o755); err != nil {
1173					return err
1174				}
1175				if err := os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(opts.SessionLogDir, simulatedSessionFile), []byte(`{"event":"started"}`+"\n"), 0o644); err != nil {
1176					return err
1177				}
1178	
1179				// Capture worktree root listing after simulated writes.
1180				entries, err = os.ReadDir(opts.WorkDir)
1181				if err != nil {
1182					return err
1183				}
1184				for _, e := range entries {
1185					wtRootAfter = append(wtRootAfter, e.Name())
1186				}
1187				return nil
1188			},
1189		}
1190		f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
1191	
1192		res := runExecuteBead(t, f, git, "my-bead", "--harness", "agent")
1193		require.Equal(t, "merged", res.Outcome, "execute-bead should succeed for this test")
1194	
1195		// The runner must have received a SessionLogDir override.
1196		require.NotEmpty(t, sessionLogDirSeen, "execute-bead must pass a SessionLogDir to the embedded harness")
1197	
1198		// The override must point inside the tracked execution bundle, not at
1199		// the worktree root.
1200		require.NotEmpty(t, res.ExecutionDir, "execute-bead must record an execution bundle dir")
1201		bundleAbs := filepath.Join(f.WorkingDir, filepath.FromSlash(res.ExecutionDir))
1202		absLog, err := filepath.Abs(sessionLogDirSeen)
1203		require.NoError(t, err)
1204		absBundle, err := filepath.Abs(bundleAbs)
1205		require.NoError(t, err)
1206		assert.Truef(t, strings.HasPrefix(absLog, absBundle+string(filepath.Separator)),
1207			"SessionLogDir (%s) must be inside the execution bundle (%s)", absLog, absBundle)
1208		assert.Equal(t, "embedded", filepath.Base(absLog),
1209			"SessionLogDir must be the bundle's embedded/ subdirectory")
1210	
1211		// The worktree root must not gain any files during the run.
1212		require.NotEmpty(t, wtPathDuringRun)
1213		assert.Equal(t, wtRootBefore, wtRootAfter,
1214			"worktree root entries must not change while the embedded harness runs (before=%v after=%v)",
1215			wtRootBefore, wtRootAfter)
1216		assert.NotContains(t, wtRootAfter, simulatedSessionFile,
1217			"simulated embedded session file must not land at the worktree root")
1218		assert.NotContains(t, wtRootAfter, ".agent-session.json",
1219			"embedded agent must not write .agent-session.json at the worktree root")
1220	
1221		// The simulated session file must exist at the redirected location.
1222		assert.FileExists(t, filepath.Join(sessionLogDirSeen, simulatedSessionFile))
1223	}

1225	// TestExecuteBeadPromptIsXMLTagged verifies that the synthesized execute-bead
1226	// prompt is emitted as a well-structured XML document with the tags required

1239			Acceptance:  "Prompt is XML-tagged with <execute-bead>, <bead>, <governing>, and <instructions>.",
1240			Labels:      []string{"area:agent", "area:docs"},

1243		specPath := filepath.Join(workDir, "docs", "feat-xml.md")

1245		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(specPath, []byte(`---
1246	ddx:
1247	  id: FEAT-XML-TEST
1248	  title: XML Test Spec
1249	---
1250	# XML Test Spec
1251	`), 0o644))
1252	
1253		git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
1254			mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111cafe",
1255			wtHeadRev:   "bbbb2222beef",

1258		f := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
1259		seedDefaultExecuteBeads(t, workDir)
1260		f.AgentRunnerOverride = runner
1261		f.executeBeadGitOverride = git
1262		f.executeBeadOrchestratorGitOverride = git

1265		_ = runExecuteBead(t, f, git, "xml-bead")
1266	
1267		require.NotEmpty(t, runner.last.PromptFile)
1268		promptRaw, err := os.ReadFile(runner.last.PromptFile)
1269		require.NoError(t, err)
1270		promptText := string(promptRaw)
1271	
1272		// Required root and subsection tags.
1273		assert.Contains(t, promptText, "<execute-bead>")
1274		assert.Contains(t, promptText, "</execute-bead>")
1275		assert.Contains(t, promptText, `<bead id="xml-bead">`)

1282		assert.Contains(t, promptText, "<labels>area:agent, area:docs</labels>")
1283		assert.Contains(t, promptText, `parent="ddx-parent"`)
1284		assert.Contains(t, promptText, `spec-id="FEAT-XML-TEST"`)
1285		assert.Contains(t, promptText, `base-rev="aaaa1111cafe"`)
1286		assert.Contains(t, promptText, `<metadata `)
1287		assert.Contains(t, promptText, "<governing>")
1288		assert.Contains(t, promptText, "</governing>")
1289		assert.Contains(t, promptText, `<ref id="FEAT-XML-TEST"`)
1290		assert.Contains(t, promptText, "<instructions>")
1291		assert.Contains(t, promptText, "</instructions>")
1292	
1293		// Regression guard: no markdown-heading-only sections.
1294		assert.NotContains(t, promptText, "# Execute Bead\n")
1295		assert.NotContains(t, promptText, "## Bead\n")
1296		assert.NotContains(t, promptText, "## Description\n")
1297		assert.NotContains(t, promptText, "## Acceptance Criteria\n")

1301		// The prompt must be parseable as a well-formed XML document.
1302		decoder := xml.NewDecoder(bytes.NewBufferString(promptText))
1303		for {

22	// scrubbedGitEnvInteg returns the current environment with all GIT_* variables
23	// removed, ensuring test-local git subprocesses don't inherit parent repo state.
24	func scrubbedGitEnvInteg() []string {
25		parent := os.Environ()
26		env := make([]string, 0, len(parent))
27		for _, kv := range parent {
28			if strings.HasPrefix(kv, "GIT_") {
29				continue
30			}
31			env = append(env, kv)
32		}
33		return env
34	}
35	
36	// runGitInteg runs a git command in dir with scrubbed GIT_* env.
37	// Fails the test on non-zero exit.
38	func runGitInteg(t *testing.T, dir string, args ...string) string {
39		t.Helper()
40		cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
41		cmd.Dir = dir
42		cmd.Env = scrubbedGitEnvInteg()
43		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
44		if err != nil {
45			t.Fatalf("git %v in %s: %v\n%s", args, dir, err, out)
46		}
47		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
48	}
49	
50	// runGitIntegOutput runs a git command and returns (output, error) — for cases
51	// where failure is expected or handled by the caller.
52	func runGitIntegOutput(dir string, args ...string) (string, error) {
53		cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
54		cmd.Dir = dir
55		cmd.Env = scrubbedGitEnvInteg()
56		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
57		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err
58	}

64	// newScriptHarnessRepo creates a temp git repo with an initial seed commit on
65	// main, seeds .ddx/beads.jsonl with beadCount open beads (IDs like
66	// ddx-int-0001, ddx-int-0002 …), and returns (projectRoot, initialSHA).
67	// The initialSHA is the SHA immediately after the "chore: initial seed" commit,
68	// BEFORE the seed-beads commit, so callers can measure commits added beyond it.
69	func newScriptHarnessRepo(t *testing.T, beadCount int) (string, string) {
70		t.Helper()
71	
72		root := t.TempDir()
73	
74		runGitInteg(t, root, "init", "-b", "main")
75		runGitInteg(t, root, "config", "user.email", "test@ddx.test")
76		runGitInteg(t, root, "config", "user.name", "DDx Test")
77	
78		// Create initial seed file and commit so the repo has a HEAD.
79		seedFile := filepath.Join(root, "seed.txt")
80		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(seedFile, []byte("seed\n"), 0644))
81		runGitInteg(t, root, "add", ".")
82		runGitInteg(t, root, "commit", "-m", "chore: initial seed")
83	
84		initialSHA := runGitInteg(t, root, "rev-parse", "HEAD")
85	
86		// Set up .ddx dir and bead store.
87		ddxDir := filepath.Join(root, ".ddx")
88		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0755))
89		store := bead.NewStore(ddxDir)
90		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
91	
92		for i := 0; i < beadCount; i++ {
93			id := fmt.Sprintf("ddx-int-%04d", i+1)
94			b := &bead.Bead{
95				ID:        id,
96				Title:     fmt.Sprintf("Integration test bead %d", i+1),
97				IssueType: "task",
98				Priority:  i,
99			}
100			require.NoError(t, store.Create(b))
101		}
102	
103		// Commit beads.jsonl so the initial worktree contains it.
104		runGitInteg(t, root, "add", ".ddx/beads.jsonl")
105		runGitInteg(t, root, "commit", "-m", "chore: seed beads")
106	
107		return root, initialSHA
108	}

123	// Executor helpers
124	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
125	
126	// landSerializerMu guards landSerializerMap.
127	var (
128		landSerializerMu  sync.Mutex
129		landSerializerMap = map[string]*sync.Mutex{}
130	)
131	
132	// landMutexFor returns the per-projectRoot mutex that serializes Land() calls.
133	// This mimics the production per-project LandCoordinator goroutine
134	// (cli/internal/server/workers.go). Tests that spawn concurrent workers share
135	// this mutex so the CAS-based UpdateRefTo in Land() never races.
136	func landMutexFor(projectRoot string) *sync.Mutex {
137		landSerializerMu.Lock()
138		defer landSerializerMu.Unlock()
139		m, ok := landSerializerMap[projectRoot]
140		if !ok {
141			m = &sync.Mutex{}
142			landSerializerMap[projectRoot] = m
143		}
144		return m
145	}
146	
147	// scriptHarnessExecutor builds an ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc that:
148	//  1. Runs ExecuteBead with the script harness (directive file = directivePath).
149	//  2. Passes the result through LandBeadResult with a LandingAdvancer that
150	//     calls Land() — serialized via landMutexFor(projectRoot).
151	//
152	// Both ExecuteBead and Land() are serialized per projectRoot. This is required
153	// because ExecuteBead calls CommitTracker (which acquires the git index lock)
154	// and because Land() uses CAS-based UpdateRefTo. In production, the server
155	// dispatches one bead per worker and serializes Land() via the LandCoordinator
156	// goroutine; in integration tests, the same mutex handles both.
157	//
158	// Failed exits (exit code != 0) with commits are preserved under
159	// refs/ddx/iterations/ by LandBeadResult before Land() is invoked.
160	func scriptHarnessExecutor(t *testing.T, projectRoot, directivePath string) ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc {
161		t.Helper()
162		// Build Runner once at executor-construction time so concurrent goroutine
163		// calls do not race on BuiltinCatalog (NewRunner is not goroutine-safe
164		// during catalog construction).
165		runner := NewRunner(Config{})
166		gitOps := &RealGitOps{}
167		orchGitOps := &RealOrchestratorGitOps{}
168		// Per-projectRoot mutex serializes git operations so concurrent workers
169		// don't race on the git index or on Land()'s CAS UpdateRefTo.
170		repoMu := landMutexFor(projectRoot)
171	
172		return ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
173			repoMu.Lock()
174			defer repoMu.Unlock()
175	
176			res, err := ExecuteBead(ctx, projectRoot, beadID, ExecuteBeadOptions{
177				Harness:     "script",
178				Model:       directivePath,
179				AgentRunner: runner,
180			}, gitOps)
181			if err != nil {
182				if res != nil {
183					return executeBeadResultToReport(res), nil
184				}
185				return ExecuteBeadReport{
186					BeadID: beadID,
187					Status: ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
188					Detail: err.Error(),
189				}, nil
190			}
191	
192			landing, landErr := LandBeadResult(projectRoot, res, orchGitOps, BeadLandingOptions{
193				LandingAdvancer: func(r *ExecuteBeadResult) (*LandResult, error) {
194					// landMutexFor is already held (repoMu == landMutexFor(projectRoot)).
195					req := BuildLandRequestFromResult(projectRoot, r)
196					return Land(projectRoot, req, RealLandingGitOps{})
197				},
198			})
199			if landErr != nil {
200				return ExecuteBeadReport{
201					BeadID: beadID,
202					Status: ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
203					Detail: landErr.Error(),
204				}, nil
205			}
206			ApplyLandingToResult(res, landing)
207			return executeBeadResultToReport(res), nil
208		})
209	}
210	
211	// executeBeadResultToReport converts ExecuteBeadResult to ExecuteBeadReport.
212	func executeBeadResultToReport(res *ExecuteBeadResult) ExecuteBeadReport {
213		return ExecuteBeadReport{
214			BeadID:             res.BeadID,
215			AttemptID:          res.AttemptID,
216			WorkerID:           res.WorkerID,
217			Harness:            res.Harness,
218			Model:              res.Model,
219			Status:             res.Status,
220			Detail:             res.Detail,
221			SessionID:          res.SessionID,
222			BaseRev:            res.BaseRev,
223			ResultRev:          res.ResultRev,
224			PreserveRef:        res.PreserveRef,
225			NoChangesRationale: res.NoChangesRationale,
226		}
227	}

1	package agent
2	
3	// execute_bead_land.go — the land coordinator pattern.
4	//
5	// This file implements the human-PR landing model for execute-bead results.
6	// The old Merge() path in execute_bead_orchestrator.go has been deleted; all
7	// target-ref writes now flow through Land(), which is called exactly once per
8	// submission by a per-project coordinator goroutine (see
9	// internal/server/workers.go:LandCoordinator).
10	//
11	// The flow mirrors how a human merges PRs:
12	//
13	//   1. Fetch the current target tip (from origin when a remote exists, or
14	//      from the local branch otherwise).
15	//   2. If the current tip equals the worker's BaseRev — fast-forward the
16	//      target branch directly to the worker's ResultRev via update-ref. The
17	//      worker's commit keeps its original parent; no new commit is created.
18	//   3. Otherwise — the target has advanced since the worker started. Create
19	//      a temporary detached worktree at the current target tip, run
20	//      `git merge --no-ff` to introduce the worker's ResultRev, and
21	//      fast-forward the target branch to the resulting merge commit. The
22	//      worker's commit is NEVER rewritten: its parent is still BaseRev, so
23	//      a later replay observes the same inputs the worker saw.
24	//   4. If the merge conflicts — abort cleanly, preserve the original
25	//      ResultRev under refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/<attempt-id>-<short-tip>,
26	//      and return preserved status. Target branch is never modified.
27	//   5. If an origin remote exists — push the new target tip. The push is
28	//      strictly fast-forward; push failures are reported via PushFailed but
29	//      do not roll back the local target ref.
30	//
31	// Replay fidelity is the reason for merge-over-rebase: a rebased commit has
32	// a rewritten parent that lies about what the worker saw at execution time.
33	// A merge commit preserves both histories — the worker's original commit
34	// (parent = BaseRev) and the target's prior tip — losslessly.
35	//
36	// The coordinator owning the goroutine provides the serialization guarantee,
37	// so Land() itself does not take any locks.
38	
39	import (
40		"fmt"
41		"os"
42		osexec "os/exec"
43		"path/filepath"
44		"strings"
45	)
46	
47	// LandRequest is one submission to the land coordinator: "here is the worker's
48	// result from base B to result R for bead X; land it on the project's target
49	// branch."
50	type LandRequest struct {
51		// WorktreeDir is the path to the project's repository directory (the
52		// directory the coordinator operates on). The original worker worktree
53		// has typically already been removed by the time Land() runs — Land()
54		// creates its own temporary worktrees when a merge is needed.
55		WorktreeDir string
56	
57		// BaseRev is the revision the worker branched off when it started.
58		// When the current target tip equals BaseRev, Land() takes the fast path.
59		BaseRev string
60	
61		// ResultRev is the worker's final commit SHA. Must be reachable in the
62		// project's git object store at the time Land() is called.
63		ResultRev string
64	
65		// BeadID identifies the bead this submission is for. Used to build the
66		// preserve-ref path on conflict.
67		BeadID string
68	
69		// AttemptID uniquely identifies this land attempt. Used to build the
70		// preserve-ref path on conflict so concurrent attempts for the same
71		// bead do not collide.
72		AttemptID string
73	
74		// TargetBranch is the branch to advance. When empty, Land() resolves
75		// the project's current HEAD branch and uses that.
76		TargetBranch string
77	
78		// EvidenceDir is the relative path to the per-attempt execution evidence
79		// directory (e.g. ".ddx/executions/20260416T181205-b5419982"). When
80		// non-empty and the main land succeeds, Land() creates a trailing
81		// evidence commit that folds these files into the target branch. The
82		// agent's commit SHA is NOT amended — the evidence commit is a separate
83		// child commit, preserving closing_commit_sha references.
84		EvidenceDir string
85	}
86	
87	// LandResult describes the outcome of a single Land() call.
88	type LandResult struct {
89		// Status is one of:
90		//   - "landed":    the target branch now points at a new commit
91		//                  (either ResultRev itself on the fast-forward path,
92		//                  or a merge commit on the merge path).
93		//   - "preserved": the merge conflicted; ResultRev is saved under
94		//                  PreserveRef and the target branch is unchanged.
95		//   - "no-changes": ResultRev == BaseRev; nothing to land.
96		Status string
97	
98		// NewTip is the new value of the target branch when Status == "landed".
99		// On the fast-forward path NewTip == ResultRev; on the merge path NewTip
100		// is the SHA of the merge commit (whose parents are the prior target
101		// tip and ResultRev). Empty when preserved or no-changes.
102		NewTip string
103	
104		// Merged is true when the land took the merge-commit path (current tip
105		// had advanced beyond BaseRev, so Land() created a merge commit to
106		// combine the worker's result with the new target tip). False on the
107		// fast-forward path where the worker's commit became the new tip
108		// unchanged.
109		Merged bool
110	
111		// PreserveRef is set when Status == "preserved". It names the ref under
112		// refs/ddx/iterations/ where ResultRev was saved for later recovery.
113		PreserveRef string
114	
115		// Reason is a human-readable explanation, especially useful when
116		// Status == "preserved" (e.g. "merge conflict") or when PushFailed.
117		Reason string
118	
119		// PushFailed is true when the local target ref was advanced successfully
120		// but the subsequent push to origin was rejected (e.g. non-fast-forward).
121		// The local state is authoritative; the remote will need to be
122		// reconciled by a later land or an operator.
123		PushFailed bool
124	
125		// PushError captures the underlying push error when PushFailed is true.
126		PushError string
127	
128		// MergedCommitCount is the number of commits reachable from ResultRev but
129		// not from BaseRev — i.e. the "size" of the worker's contribution being
130		// merged in. Zero on the no-changes path. Set on both the fast-forward
131		// and merge-commit paths so metrics can compare contribution sizes.
132		MergedCommitCount int
133	
134		// EvidenceCommitSHA is set when a trailing execution-evidence commit was
135		// created after the main land. When set, NewTip points at this commit
136		// (not at the original agent commit or merge commit).
137		EvidenceCommitSHA string
138	}
139	
140	// PreClaimResult is the outcome of a FetchOriginAncestryCheck call.
141	type PreClaimResult struct {
142		// Action is one of:
143		//   "unchanged"     — local tip == origin tip, no action taken
144		//   "fast-forwarded"— local was behind origin; local branch was advanced
145		//   "no-origin"     — no origin remote; check skipped
146		//   "local-ahead"   — local is ahead of origin; no action needed
147		//   "diverged"      — neither is ancestor of the other; claim should be aborted
148		Action    string
149		LocalSHA  string
150		OriginSHA string
151	}
152

154	// shells out to git; tests inject fakes or run against real temp repos.
155	type LandingGitOps interface {

157		HasRemote(dir, remote string) bool

217		// to the local branch tip. When origin is ahead the local branch is
218		// fast-forwarded via update-ref + read-tree. When the two tips have
219		// diverged (neither is ancestor of the other) the returned result has
220		// Action == "diverged". When no origin remote exists the result has
221		// Action == "no-origin". The caller decides whether to abort a claim.
222		FetchOriginAncestryCheck(dir, targetBranch string) (PreClaimResult, error)
223	
224		// CountCommits returns the number of commits reachable from tip but not
225		// from base (i.e. git rev-list --count base..tip). Used to record the
226		// contribution size in land metrics. Returns 0 on error.
227		CountCommits(dir, base, tip string) int
228	
229		// StageDir stages all files under relPath in dir for the next commit.
230		StageDir(dir, relPath string) error
231	
232		// CommitStaged creates a commit from currently staged changes using msg
233		// as the commit message. Returns (sha, nil) when a commit was made,
234		// ("", nil) when nothing was staged, and ("", err) on failure.
235		CommitStaged(dir, msg string) (string, error)
236	}

290	func (RealLandingGitOps) SyncWorkTreeToHead(dir, fromRev string) error {
291		// Step 1: sync the index to HEAD. This is required before checkout-index
292		// below will do anything useful, and also keeps subsequent CommitTracker
293		// calls from building stale trees.
294		out, err := osexec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "read-tree", "HEAD").CombinedOutput()
295		if err != nil {
296			return fmt.Errorf("git read-tree HEAD: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
297		}
298	
299		// Step 2: compute the list of tracked files changed between the previous
300		// HEAD and the current HEAD. These are the files that the landing commit
301		// added, modified, or deleted. We only restore THESE files to avoid
302		// clobbering unrelated local modifications (agent-logs being written by
303		// the running server, beads.jsonl heartbeat updates, operator scratch).
304		if fromRev == "" {
305			// No previous HEAD known — fall back to the unsafe behaviour of
306			// read-tree only. Acceptable when the caller is a best-effort path
307			// that cannot reconstruct fromRev.
308			return nil
309		}
310		diffOut, diffErr := osexec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "diff", "--name-only", fromRev, "HEAD").CombinedOutput()
311		if diffErr != nil {
312			// Diff failed (bad fromRev, shallow history, etc.) — leave the
313			// worktree stale rather than risk a broken checkout. The CommitTracker
314			// stale-tree bug is the prior status quo and no worse than before.
315			return nil
316		}
317		changed := strings.Fields(strings.TrimSpace(string(diffOut)))
318		if len(changed) == 0 {
319			return nil
320		}
321	
322		// Step 3: split into existing-in-HEAD (checkout-index) and
323		// deleted-in-HEAD (os.Remove) buckets. checkout-index only writes files
324		// that are in the index; it cannot represent a deletion, so we handle
325		// those ourselves.
326		var indexFiles []string
327		var removedFiles []string
328		for _, f := range changed {
329			probe := osexec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "ls-files", "--error-unmatch", "--", f)
330			if probe.Run() == nil {
331				indexFiles = append(indexFiles, f)
332			} else {
333				removedFiles = append(removedFiles, f)
334			}
335		}
336	
337		// Step 4: materialize the index-present files into the working tree.
338		// -f overwrites any stale content at these exact paths. Unrelated files
339		// are untouched because we pass the specific path list.
340		if len(indexFiles) > 0 {
341			args := []string{"-C", dir, "checkout-index", "-f", "--"}
342			args = append(args, indexFiles...)
343			out2, err2 := osexec.Command("git", args...).CombinedOutput()
344			if err2 != nil {
345				return fmt.Errorf("git checkout-index -f: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out2)), err2)
346			}
347		}
348	
349		// Step 5: remove files that the landing commit deleted and whose removal
350		// did not propagate to the worktree because update-ref bypassed the
351		// working-tree update.
352		for _, f := range removedFiles {
353			full := filepath.Join(dir, f)
354			_ = os.Remove(full) // best-effort; leave the file if removal fails
355		}
356	
357		return nil
358	}

363		if err != nil {

375	func (RealLandingGitOps) MergeInto(wtDir, srcRev, msg string) error {
376		// --no-ff forces a merge commit even when the merge could fast-forward
377		// (which shouldn't happen given our caller's ancestry check, but is a
378		// defensive guarantee that target history always gets a marker commit).
379		// We inject user.name/user.email via -c so the merge commit can be
380		// created even when the worktree inherited no git config; the
381		// coordinator is a machine actor and should not adopt a human's identity.
382		out, err := osexec.Command(
383			"git", "-C", wtDir,
384			"-c", "user.name=ddx-land-coordinator",
385			"-c", "user.email=coordinator@ddx.local",
386			"merge", "--no-ff", "-m", msg, srcRev,
387		).CombinedOutput()
388		if err != nil {
389			_ = osexec.Command("git", "-C", wtDir, "merge", "--abort").Run()
390			return fmt.Errorf("git merge --no-ff %s: %s: %w", srcRev, strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
391		}
392		return nil
393	}

404		// Refspec "<local>:<remote>" with no '+' prefix → fast-forward only.
405		refspec := fmt.Sprintf("%s:refs/heads/%s", localRef, targetBranch)
406		out, err := osexec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "push", remote, refspec).CombinedOutput()
407		if err != nil {

409		}

411	}

413	func (RealLandingGitOps) CountCommits(dir, base, tip string) int {
414		out, err := osexec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "rev-list", "--count", base+".."+tip).CombinedOutput()
415		if err != nil {
416			return 0
417		}
418		s := strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
419		n := 0
420		for _, c := range s {
421			if c >= '0' && c <= '9' {
422				n = n*10 + int(c-'0')
423			}
424		}
425		return n
426	}

433		// usage.json remain tracked for audit; the raw session log lives on
434		// disk, not in git history.

443	func (RealLandingGitOps) CommitStaged(dir, msg string) (string, error) {
444		out, _ := osexec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "diff", "--cached", "--name-only").Output()
445		if len(strings.TrimSpace(string(out))) == 0 {
446			return "", nil
447		}
448		commitOut, err := osexec.Command("git", "-C", dir,
449			"-c", "user.name=ddx-land-coordinator",
450			"-c", "user.email=coordinator@ddx.local",
451			"commit", "-m", msg,
452		).CombinedOutput()
453		if err != nil {
454			return "", fmt.Errorf("git commit: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(commitOut)), err)
455		}
456		shaOut, err := osexec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "rev-parse", "HEAD").Output()
457		if err != nil {
458			return "", fmt.Errorf("rev-parse HEAD after evidence commit: %w", err)
459		}
460		return strings.TrimSpace(string(shaOut)), nil
461	}

463	// FetchOriginAncestryCheck implements LandingGitOps.FetchOriginAncestryCheck.
464	func (RealLandingGitOps) FetchOriginAncestryCheck(dir, targetBranch string) (PreClaimResult, error) {
465		// Step 1: check for origin remote.
466		out, err := osexec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "remote", "get-url", "origin").CombinedOutput()
467		if err != nil {
468			// No origin remote — single-machine case; skip check.
469			return PreClaimResult{Action: "no-origin"}, nil
470		}
471		_ = out // remote URL not needed
472	
473		// Step 2: fetch origin/targetBranch (best-effort; failure is non-fatal but surfaced).
474		if fetchOut, fetchErr := osexec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "fetch", "origin", targetBranch).CombinedOutput(); fetchErr != nil {
475			return PreClaimResult{}, fmt.Errorf("git fetch origin %s: %s: %w", targetBranch, strings.TrimSpace(string(fetchOut)), fetchErr)
476		}
477	
478		// Step 3: resolve local tip.
479		localOut, localErr := osexec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "rev-parse", "--verify", "refs/heads/"+targetBranch).CombinedOutput()
480		if localErr != nil {
481			return PreClaimResult{}, fmt.Errorf("resolving local %s: %s: %w", targetBranch, strings.TrimSpace(string(localOut)), localErr)
482		}
483		localSHA := strings.TrimSpace(string(localOut))
484	
485		// Step 4: resolve origin tip.
486		originOut, originErr := osexec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "rev-parse", "--verify", "refs/remotes/origin/"+targetBranch).CombinedOutput()
487		if originErr != nil {
488			return PreClaimResult{}, fmt.Errorf("resolving origin/%s: %s: %w", targetBranch, strings.TrimSpace(string(originOut)), originErr)
489		}
490		originSHA := strings.TrimSpace(string(originOut))
491	
492		// Step 5: compare.
493		if localSHA == originSHA {
494			return PreClaimResult{Action: "unchanged", LocalSHA: localSHA, OriginSHA: originSHA}, nil
495		}
496	
497		// Is local an ancestor of origin? (origin is ahead)
498		localAncestorErr := osexec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "merge-base", "--is-ancestor", localSHA, originSHA).Run()
499		if localAncestorErr == nil {
500			// Origin is ahead: fast-forward local branch via update-ref + sync worktree.
501			targetRef := "refs/heads/" + targetBranch
502			if upErr := osexec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "update-ref", targetRef, originSHA, localSHA).Run(); upErr != nil {
503				return PreClaimResult{}, fmt.Errorf("fast-forwarding %s to %s: %w", targetRef, originSHA, upErr)
504			}
505			// Sync index + working tree to new HEAD so the main worktree files
506			// reflect the pulled-down origin commits. Pass localSHA as fromRev
507			// to restrict the restore to files actually changed by origin's
508			// advance, preserving unrelated local modifications.
509			_ = (RealLandingGitOps{}).SyncWorkTreeToHead(dir, localSHA)
510			return PreClaimResult{Action: "fast-forwarded", LocalSHA: localSHA, OriginSHA: originSHA}, nil
511		}
512	
513		// Is origin an ancestor of local? (local is ahead)
514		originAncestorErr := osexec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "merge-base", "--is-ancestor", originSHA, localSHA).Run()
515		if originAncestorErr == nil {
516			return PreClaimResult{Action: "local-ahead", LocalSHA: localSHA, OriginSHA: originSHA}, nil
517		}
518	
519		// Neither is ancestor of the other: diverged.
520		return PreClaimResult{Action: "diverged", LocalSHA: localSHA, OriginSHA: originSHA}, nil
521	}
522	
523	// landIterationRef returns the documented hidden ref for a land-time preserve.
524	// Format: refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/<attempt-id>-<short-tip>. The short-tip
525	// captures the current target tip at the time of the conflict so subsequent
526	// recovery tools can reconstruct which merge target was in play.
527	func landIterationRef(beadID, attemptID, tip string) string {
528		short := tip
529		if len(short) > 12 {
530			short = short[:12]
531		}
532		attempt := attemptID
533		if attempt == "" {
534			attempt = NowFunc().UTC().Format("20060102T150405Z")
535		}
536		return fmt.Sprintf("refs/ddx/iterations/%s/%s-%s", beadID, attempt, short)
537	}
538	
539	// landEvidence creates a trailing commit that folds the per-attempt execution
540	// evidence directory into the target branch. Called after the main land (ff or
541	// merge) succeeds and before the push. The evidence commit is a normal child of
542	// result.NewTip — the agent's original commit SHA is not amended.
543	//
544	// Best-effort: only works when HEAD is a symbolic ref to targetBranch (the
545	// normal case for server workers and CLI users). When HEAD is detached or
546	// points elsewhere, the evidence commit is silently skipped.
547	func landEvidence(wd, targetBranch string, req LandRequest, gitOps LandingGitOps, result *LandResult) {
548		branch, err := gitOps.CurrentBranch(wd)
549		if err != nil || branch != targetBranch {
550			return
551		}
552		if err := gitOps.StageDir(wd, req.EvidenceDir); err != nil {
553			return
554		}
555		msg := fmt.Sprintf("chore: add execution evidence [%s]", shortAttempt(req.AttemptID))
556		sha, err := gitOps.CommitStaged(wd, msg)
557		if err != nil || sha == "" {
558			return
559		}
560		result.EvidenceCommitSHA = sha
561		result.NewTip = sha
562	}
563	
564	// Land performs fetch → (ff or merge) → push for a single submission.
565	// Callers MUST serialize calls per projectRoot (the server coordinator
566	// goroutine provides this). Land() itself takes no internal locks.
567	//
568	// projectRoot is the directory containing the project's .git. req.WorktreeDir,
569	// when non-empty, is used as the git working directory for all commands; when
570	// empty, projectRoot is used. Both usually refer to the same dir — the field
571	// is kept for forward compatibility with multi-clone topologies.
572	func Land(projectRoot string, req LandRequest, gitOps LandingGitOps) (*LandResult, error) {
573		if gitOps == nil {
574			gitOps = RealLandingGitOps{}
575		}
576		wd := req.WorktreeDir
577		if wd == "" {
578			wd = projectRoot
579		}
580	
581		// Trivial guard — no commits to land.
582		if req.ResultRev == "" || req.ResultRev == req.BaseRev {
583			return &LandResult{Status: "no-changes", Reason: "result_rev == base_rev"}, nil
584		}
585	
586		// Resolve target branch (default to project HEAD).
587		targetBranch := req.TargetBranch
588		if targetBranch == "" {
589			br, err := gitOps.CurrentBranch(wd)
590			if err != nil {
591				return nil, fmt.Errorf("resolving target branch: %w", err)
592			}
593			targetBranch = br
594		}
595		targetRef := "refs/heads/" + targetBranch
596	
597		// Fetch the current target tip from origin when available; otherwise
598		// read the local branch directly.
599		hasOrigin := gitOps.HasRemote(wd, "origin")
600		if hasOrigin {
601			// Fetch is best-effort: a disconnected remote must not block
602			// a local land. Log-style errors are swallowed here; the
603			// subsequent ResolveRef will surface any fatal issue.
604			_ = gitOps.FetchBranch(wd, "origin", targetBranch)
605		}
606		currentTip, err := gitOps.ResolveRef(wd, targetRef)
607		if err != nil {
608			return nil, fmt.Errorf("resolving target tip %s: %w", targetRef, err)
609		}
610	
611		contribCount := gitOps.CountCommits(wd, req.BaseRev, req.ResultRev)
612	
613		// Fast path: no sibling advanced the target branch → straight ff via
614		// update-ref. The worker's commit becomes the new tip unchanged; its
615		// parent is still BaseRev, so replay sees the same inputs the worker
616		// saw. No merge commit is created.
617		if currentTip == req.BaseRev {
618			if err := gitOps.UpdateRefTo(wd, targetRef, req.ResultRev, currentTip); err != nil {
619				return nil, fmt.Errorf("fast-forwarding %s to %s: %w", targetRef, req.ResultRev, err)
620			}
621			// Refresh the main worktree's index + working-tree files so the
622			// operator and subsequent agents see the newly-landed commit's
623			// changes. Pass currentTip as fromRev so only files actually
624			// changed by this commit are restored — unrelated local
625			// modifications (agent-logs, beads.jsonl heartbeat) are preserved.
626			// Non-fatal on error.
627			_ = gitOps.SyncWorkTreeToHead(wd, currentTip)
628			result := &LandResult{
629				Status:            "landed",
630				NewTip:            req.ResultRev,
631				Merged:            false,
632				MergedCommitCount: contribCount,
633			}
634			if req.EvidenceDir != "" {
635				landEvidence(wd, targetBranch, req, gitOps, result)
636			}
637			landPush(wd, targetBranch, result.NewTip, hasOrigin, gitOps, result)
638			return result, nil
639		}
640	
641		// Merge path: the target has advanced since the worker started. Create
642		// a temp detached worktree at currentTip and run `git merge --no-ff
643		// ResultRev` inside it. The result is a merge commit whose parents are
644		// [currentTip, ResultRev]. Crucially, ResultRev itself is NOT rewritten:
645		// its parent is still BaseRev, so replay observes the original inputs.
646		tempWT, tempWtErr := os.MkdirTemp("", "ddx-land-wt-*")
647		if tempWtErr != nil {
648			return nil, fmt.Errorf("creating temp worktree dir: %w", tempWtErr)
649		}
650		// os.MkdirTemp creates the dir, but git worktree add refuses to run if
651		// the target already exists. Remove it first so git can recreate it.
652		_ = os.RemoveAll(tempWT)
653		if err := gitOps.AddWorktree(wd, tempWT, currentTip); err != nil {
654			return nil, fmt.Errorf("adding temp worktree at %s: %w", currentTip, err)
655		}
656		defer func() { _ = gitOps.RemoveWorktree(wd, tempWT) }()
657	
658		mergeMsg := fmt.Sprintf("Merge bead %s attempt %s into %s", req.BeadID, shortAttempt(req.AttemptID), targetBranch)
659		if err := gitOps.MergeInto(tempWT, req.ResultRev, mergeMsg); err != nil {
660			// Merge conflict: preserve the original ResultRev and return.
661			preserveRef := landIterationRef(req.BeadID, req.AttemptID, currentTip)
662			if upErr := gitOps.UpdateRefTo(wd, preserveRef, req.ResultRev, ""); upErr != nil {
663				return nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving %s after merge conflict: %w", preserveRef, upErr)
664			}
665			return &LandResult{
666				Status:            "preserved",
667				PreserveRef:       preserveRef,
668				Reason:            "merge conflict",
669				MergedCommitCount: contribCount,
670			}, nil
671		}
672	
673		// Merge clean: read the merge commit SHA from the temp worktree's HEAD
674		// and fast-forward the target branch to it.
675		mergeSHA, err := gitOps.HeadRevAt(tempWT)
676		if err != nil {
677			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reading merge HEAD: %w", err)
678		}
679		if err := gitOps.UpdateRefTo(wd, targetRef, mergeSHA, currentTip); err != nil {
680			return nil, fmt.Errorf("fast-forwarding %s to merge commit %s: %w", targetRef, mergeSHA, err)
681		}
682		// Refresh the main worktree's index + working-tree files. Pass
683		// currentTip as fromRev; the merge commit's tree contains both the
684		// current-tip files and the incoming ResultRev files, so the diff
685		// against currentTip yields exactly the files affected by the merge.
686		// Unrelated local modifications are preserved.
687		_ = gitOps.SyncWorkTreeToHead(wd, currentTip)
688	
689		result := &LandResult{
690			Status:            "landed",
691			NewTip:            mergeSHA,
692			Merged:            true,
693			MergedCommitCount: contribCount,
694		}
695		if req.EvidenceDir != "" {
696			landEvidence(wd, targetBranch, req, gitOps, result)
697		}
698		landPush(wd, targetBranch, result.NewTip, hasOrigin, gitOps, result)
699		return result, nil
700	}
701	
702	// landPush pushes the new target tip to origin when a remote exists. Push
703	// failure is non-fatal for the local land outcome; it is surfaced via
704	// PushFailed/PushError on the result.
705	func landPush(wd, targetBranch, newTip string, hasOrigin bool, gitOps LandingGitOps, result *LandResult) {
706		if !hasOrigin {
707			return
708		}
709		if err := gitOps.PushFFOnly(wd, "origin", newTip, targetBranch); err != nil {
710			result.PushFailed = true
711			result.PushError = err.Error()
712		}
713	}

715	// shortAttempt returns a 10-char slug derived from attemptID for use in temp
716	// branch names. When attemptID is empty, it returns the current timestamp.
717	func shortAttempt(attemptID string) string {
718		if attemptID != "" {
719			if len(attemptID) > 16 {
720				return attemptID[:16]
721			}
722			return attemptID
723		}
724		return NowFunc().UTC().Format("20060102T150405")
725	}
726	
727	// ApplyLandResultToExecuteBeadResult maps a LandResult onto an
728	// ExecuteBeadResult so the execute-bead loop sees the correct final status.
729	// It is the coordinator-pattern replacement for ApplyLandingToResult.
730	func ApplyLandResultToExecuteBeadResult(res *ExecuteBeadResult, land *LandResult) {
731		if land == nil || res == nil {
732			return
733		}
734		switch land.Status {
735		case "landed":
736			// Fast-forward or merge commit — either way the target branch now
737			// contains the worker's result. ResultRev's own parent is still
738			// BaseRev so replay fidelity is preserved.
739			res.Outcome = "merged"
740			res.Reason = ""
741			res.PreserveRef = ""
742			if land.Merged {
743				res.Reason = "merged onto current tip"
744			}
745			if land.PushFailed {
746				res.Reason = "landed locally; push failed: " + land.PushError
747			}
748			// NewTip reflects the ref actually on the target branch (either
749			// ResultRev on the ff path or the merge commit SHA on the merge path).
750			if land.NewTip != "" {
751				res.ResultRev = land.NewTip
752			}
753		case "preserved":
754			res.Outcome = "preserved"
755			res.Reason = land.Reason
756			res.PreserveRef = land.PreserveRef
757		case "no-changes":
758			// Only overwrite when the worker itself did not already report
759			// a richer no-changes rationale.
760			if res.Outcome == "" || res.Outcome == ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded {
761				res.Outcome = "no-changes"
762			}
763			if res.Reason == "" {
764				res.Reason = land.Reason
765			}
766		}
767		// Re-classify loop-visible status from the landing outcome.
768		reasonForStatus := res.Reason
769		if land.Status == "preserved" {
770			// Route preserve reasons through the land-conflict classifier so the
771			// loop sees land_conflict (not generic success).
772			reasonForStatus = "merge conflict"
773		}
774		res.Status = ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(res.Outcome, res.ExitCode, reasonForStatus)
775		res.Detail = ExecuteBeadStatusDetail(res.Status, res.Reason, res.Error)
776	}
777	
778	// BuildLandRequestFromResult constructs a LandRequest for the coordinator from
779	// an ExecuteBeadResult. The coordinator always passes projectRoot as the
780	// workdir — the worker's original worktree has already been cleaned up by the
781	// time Land() runs.
782	func BuildLandRequestFromResult(projectRoot string, res *ExecuteBeadResult) LandRequest {
783		return LandRequest{
784			WorktreeDir:  projectRoot,
785			BaseRev:      res.BaseRev,
786			ResultRev:    res.ResultRev,
787			BeadID:       res.BeadID,
788			AttemptID:    res.AttemptID,
789			TargetBranch: "",
790			EvidenceDir:  res.ExecutionDir,
791		}
792	}

25		Version string

27		Date    string

30		WorkingDir string
31	
32		// AgentRunnerOverride overrides the agent runner used by execStore (for testing).
33		AgentRunnerOverride ddxexec.AgentRunner

35		// executeBeadGitOverride overrides git operations in execute-bead worker (for testing).

40		executeBeadOrchestratorGitOverride agent.OrchestratorGitOps

44		executeBeadLandingGitOverride agent.LandingGitOps

103	func (f *CommandFactory) NewRootCommand() *cobra.Command {
104		// Local flag variables scoped to this command instance
105		var cfgFile string
106		var verbose bool
107		var libraryPath string
108	
109		// Create fresh root command
110		rootCmd := &cobra.Command{
111			Use:   "ddx",
112			Short: "Document-Driven Development eXperience - AI development toolkit",
113			Long: color.New(color.FgCyan).Sprint(banner) + `
114	DDx is a toolkit for AI-assisted development that helps you:
115	
116	• Share templates, prompts, and patterns across projects
117	• Maintain consistent development practices
118	• Integrate AI tooling seamlessly
119	• Contribute improvements back to the community
120	
121	Get started:
122	  ddx init          Initialize DDx in your project
123	  ddx list          See available resources
124	  ddx doctor        Check installation and diagnose issues
125	
126	More information:
127	  Documentation: https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx
128	  Issues & Support: https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/issues`,
129			PersistentPreRun: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) {
130				if verbose {
131					fmt.Printf("DDx %s (commit: %s, built: %s)\n", f.Version, f.Commit, f.Date)
132				}
133			},
134		}
135	
136		// Setup flags - these are now local to this command instance
137		rootCmd.PersistentFlags().StringVar(&cfgFile, "config", "", "config file (default is $HOME/.ddx.yml)")
138		rootCmd.PersistentFlags().BoolVarP(&verbose, "verbose", "v", false, "verbose output")
139		rootCmd.PersistentFlags().StringVar(&libraryPath, "library-base-path", "", "override path for DDx library location")
140	
141		// Store flag values in command context for access by subcommands
142		rootCmd.PersistentPreRunE = func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
143			// Initialize config with the local viper instance
144			f.initConfig(cfgFile, libraryPath)
145	
146			// Version gate: block old binary from running in newer project
147			if err := f.checkVersionGate(cmd); err != nil {
148				return err
149			}
150	
151			// Check for updates (synchronous, once per 24h)
152			f.checkForUpdates(cmd)
153	
154			// Call the original PersistentPreRun if it exists
155			if rootCmd.PersistentPreRun != nil {
156				rootCmd.PersistentPreRun(cmd, args)
157			}
158			return nil
159		}
160	
161		// Display update notification and staleness hints after command completes
162		rootCmd.PersistentPostRunE = func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
163			if err := f.displayUpdateNotification(cmd); err != nil {
164				return err
165			}
166			f.displayStalenessHints(cmd)
167			return nil
168		}
169	
170		// Add all subcommands
171		f.registerSubcommands(rootCmd)
172	
173		return rootCmd
174	}

176	// initConfig initializes configuration for this command instance
177	func (f *CommandFactory) initConfig(cfgFile, libPath string) {
178		// Store library path override if provided
179		if libPath != "" {
180			_ = os.Setenv("DDX_LIBRARY_BASE_PATH", libPath)
181		}
182	
183		if cfgFile != "" {
184			// Use config file from the flag
185			f.viperInstance.SetConfigFile(cfgFile)
186		} else {
187			// Find home directory
188			home, err := os.UserHomeDir()
189			if err == nil {
190				// Search for config in home directory with name ".ddx" (without extension)
191				f.viperInstance.AddConfigPath(home)
192				f.viperInstance.AddConfigPath(".")
193				f.viperInstance.SetConfigType("yaml")
194				f.viperInstance.SetConfigName(".ddx")
195			}
196		}
197	
198		f.viperInstance.AutomaticEnv() // read in environment variables that match
199	
200		// If a config file is found, read it in
201		if err := f.viperInstance.ReadInConfig(); err == nil {
202			if verbose := f.viperInstance.GetBool("verbose"); verbose {
203				_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "Using config file:", f.viperInstance.ConfigFileUsed())
204			}
205		}
206	}

208	// checkForUpdates performs automatic update check (synchronous, once per 24h)
209	func (f *CommandFactory) checkForUpdates(cmd *cobra.Command) {
210		// Check if disabled via env var
211		if os.Getenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK") == "1" {
212			return
213		}
214	
215		// Load config
216		cfg, err := config.Load()
217		if err != nil {
218			// Silent failure - use defaults
219			cfg = config.DefaultNewConfig()
220		}
221	
222		// Check if disabled via config
223		if cfg.UpdateCheck != nil && !cfg.UpdateCheck.Enabled {
224			return
225		}
226	
227		// Create checker and perform check (synchronous)
228		checker := update.NewChecker(f.Version, cfg)
229		f.updateMu.Lock()
230		f.updateChecker = checker
231		f.updateDone = make(chan struct{})
232		done := f.updateDone
233		f.updateMu.Unlock()
234	
235		// Run the check asynchronously so command startup is never blocked.
236		go func() {
237			defer close(done)
238			_, _ = checker.CheckForUpdate(context.Background())
239			// Also refresh plugin version cache while we have a network call in flight.
240			f.refreshPluginVersionCache()
241		}()
242	}

244	// displayUpdateNotification shows update notification if available
245	func (f *CommandFactory) displayUpdateNotification(cmd *cobra.Command) error {
246		// Skip if disabled via environment variable
247		if os.Getenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK") == "1" {
248			return nil
249		}
250	
251		f.updateMu.Lock()
252		checker := f.updateChecker
253		done := f.updateDone
254		f.updateMu.Unlock()
255	
256		if checker == nil {
257			return nil
258		}
259	
260		if done != nil {
261			select {
262			case <-done:
263			default:
264				return nil
265			}
266		}
267	
268		// Skip update notification for help commands
269		if cmd.Name() == "help" || cmd.Parent() != nil && cmd.Parent().Name() == "help" {
270			return nil
271		}
272	
273		// Skip update notification for machine-readable output formats
274		jsonFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
275		if jsonFlag {
276			return nil
277		}
278	
279		// Skip update notification if silent flag is set
280		silentFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("silent")
281		if silentFlag {
282			return nil
283		}
284	
285		// Skip update notification if --no-check flag is set (for version command)
286		noCheck, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-check")
287		if noCheck {
288			return nil
289		}
290	
291		// Skip when stdout is not a terminal (pipes, scripts, CI)
292		if !isatty.IsTerminal(os.Stdout.Fd()) && !isatty.IsCygwinTerminal(os.Stdout.Fd()) {
293			return nil
294		}
295	
296		available, version, err := checker.IsUpdateAvailable()
297		if err != nil || !available {
298			if err != nil {
299				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: Could not check for updates: %v\n", err)
300			}

// TestExecuteBeadMerge verifies that when merge succeeds the outcome is "merged".
func TestExecuteBeadMerge(t *testing.T) {
	git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
		mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111",
		wtHeadRev:   "bbbb2222", // agent made a new commit
		mergeErr:    nil,        // merge succeeds
	}
	runner := &fakeAgentRunner{result: &agent.Result{ExitCode: 0, Harness: "mock"}}
	f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)

	res := runExecuteBead(t, f, git, "my-bead")

	assert.Equal(t, "merged", res.Outcome)
	assert.Equal(t, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess, res.Status)
	assert.Equal(t, "aaaa1111", res.BaseRev)
	assert.Equal(t, "bbbb2222", res.ResultRev)
	assert.Empty(t, res.PreserveRef)
	assert.Equal(t, "my-bead", res.BeadID)
	assert.NotEmpty(t, res.SessionID)

	// Worktree should have been created and cleaned up.
	require.Len(t, git.addedWTs, 1)
	assert.Contains(t, git.addedWTs[0], agent.ExecuteBeadWtPrefix+"my-bead-")
	require.Len(t, git.removedWTs, 1)
	assert.Equal(t, git.addedWTs[0], git.removedWTs[0])
	assert.Equal(t, 1, git.mergeCalls)
	assert.Equal(t, "bbbb2222", git.mergeRev)
}
